session_focus: Verify that git tracks all AI-generated memory artifacts since the human user does none of the work and AI generates everything.
user_directives: User asked how to make git track the memory since AI is doing it all.
User stated there is no need to differentiate any of the AI-generated content.
User emphasized 'i the user do none' - meaning all work is AI-generated.
User wanted all artifacts tracked without manual categorization.
assistant_responses: Checked .gitignore which only excludes vault credentials and security logs.
Ran git ls-files to verify all data/, vector_store/, iterations/, MEMORY.md, retrieval_log.jsonl are tracked.
Confirmed 16 session/miss JSONL files, 47 vector entries, 31 iteration files all under version control.
Verified system already configured correctly - no changes needed.
training_value: When AI generates all work, git should track everything by default with minimal exclusions.
Only security-sensitive data (credentials, security audit logs) should be excluded from version control.
Memory artifacts, training data, vector embeddings, and iteration records are all valuable training signal and should be preserved.
The .gitignore should be minimal: only exclude what would compromise security if committed.
